tool_input.summary: To constrain the age of fluvial depositional systems in Arabia Terra, researchers typically combine (1) global/regional topography for drainage/basin geometry and stratigraphic context, (2) high-resolution regional imagery/DTMs (often HRSC on the GEO node; CTX/HiRISE are usually from the Imaging node), and sometimes (3) CRISM spectroscopy and TES thermal inertia to characterize depositional materials. On the GEO node, the most directly relevant inspected datasets are MOLA gridded topography, HRSC DTMs/map-projected images, TES thermal inertia maps, and CRISM targeted calibrated TRDR products (PDS4 collections within the legacy volumes).

candidates.title: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER PEDR AND EGDR ARCHIVES
candidates.reasoning: Primary global gridded topography (MEGDR) used to map basin/valley morphometry and regional context for Arabia Terra fluvial systems; often a base layer for geomorphic interpretation and age/stratigraphic relations.

candidates.title: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER IEGDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Interpolated/derived MOLA gridded products complementary to MEGDR; commonly used for regional elevation analysis supporting stratigraphic/geomorphic interpretation of fluvial deposits.

candidates.title: MARS EXPRESS HRSC DATA
candidates.reasoning: HRSC digital terrain models (DTMs) provide higher-resolution topography than MOLA in many areas; useful for identifying/quantifying fluvial depositional system relief (inverted channels, fans/deltas) in Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MARS EXPRESS HRSC DATA
candidates.reasoning: Map-projected HRSC reduced data products used for geomorphic mapping and (in some studies) crater counting on/around fluvial deposits to infer activity timing.

candidates.title: MARS GLOBAL SURVEYOR TES-DERIVED THERMAL INERTIA
candidates.reasoning: TES-derived thermal inertia maps help distinguish sedimentary units and mantling materials; sometimes used to support interpretation of fluvial/lacustrine deposits and resurfacing relevant to age constraints.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM TRDR Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated targeted CRISM TRDR collection (PDS4) used to identify aqueous alteration minerals associated with fluvial/lacustrine deposits in regions like Arabia Terra; mineral stratigraphy can support relative age/activity interpretations.

candidates.title: CRISM ATO S-Band LMLP Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Derived CRISM map-projected products (LMLP) can be used as enhanced spectral/mineral maps to characterize depositional systems and diagenesis tied to the timing of aqueous activity.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Geosciences node at https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/.

Directory layout:
  mission/ → dataset_or_bundle/ → volume/ (PDS3) or sub-collections (PDS4)

Top-level mission directories: m2020, insight, msl, mro, mer, mex, ody, phx, mgs, mpf, viking, mariner, mars, mgn, premgn, venus, messenger, grail, clps, lunar, lro, earth, lab, near.

Common mission/instrument abbreviations:
  Mars Express = MEX (instruments: HRSC, OMEGA, MARSIS, PFS, SPICAM, MaRS)
  Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter = MRO (instruments: HiRISE, CTX, CRISM, SHARAD, MCS)
  Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity = MSL (instruments: ChemCam, APXS, CheMin, SAM, Mastcam, MAHLI, DAN)
  Mars 2020 / Perseverance = M2020 (instruments: PIXL, SHERLOC, Mastcam-Z, SuperCam, RIMFAX)
  Mars Exploration Rovers (Spirit=MER2, Opportunity=MER1) (instruments: Pancam, Mini-TES/MTES, APXS, MB, MI)
  Mars Global Surveyor = MGS (instruments: MOC, MOLA, TES, MAG)
  Mars Odyssey = ODY (instruments: THEMIS, GRS, NS)
  Phoenix = PHX (instruments: TEGA, MECA, SSI, OM, RAC)
  Viking = VL1/VL2/VO1/VO2 (instruments: camera, IRTM, MAWD)
  MESSENGER = MESS (instruments: MDIS, GRNS, XRS, MLA, MASCS)
  Magellan = MGN (instruments: SAR, altimetry, radiometry, emissivity)
  LRO (instruments: LOLA, Diviner, LROC, Mini-RF, LAMP)
  GRAIL (instruments: LGRS)
  NEAR (instruments: NLR, MSI, XGRS, MAG)

PDS3 vs PDS4: both formats co-exist on the same node. PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style identifiers; PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`.

Bundle vs collection (PDS4): a bundle directory holds the bundle label plus sub-directories containing collection labels. `inspect_with_collections` returns both levels.

Extracting dataset_id from labels:
  - PDS3: VOLUME.DATA_SET_ID
  - PDS4 bundle: Identification_Area.logical_identifier (in `labels`)
  - PDS4 collection: Identification_Area.logical_identifier (in `collections`)

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires you to return dataset candidates. Queries come from published scientific papers and always imply specific PDS datasets that the researchers used. NEVER dismiss a query as 'interpretation', 'conceptual', or 'not a dataset question'. Even if the question sounds abstract or science-focused, your job is to identify which PDS datasets would be needed to answer it. Always return at least one candidate.

  2. When search results return a high-scoring match (score >= 80), strongly prefer it as a candidate. Do NOT ignore high-score results in favor of lower-scoring or unrelated datasets.

  3. Prefer CALIBRATED and REDUCED data products over raw/EDR data when the query implies scientific analysis. Scientists typically use calibrated (RDR, CDR) or derived (DDR) products. For MER instruments, prefer the PDS4 calibrated collections (e.g. mer2_pancam_sci_calibrated2, mer2_mi_sci_calibrated, mer2_mtes_calibrated_radiance) over raw EDR data. Search for both 'calibrated' and the instrument name.

  4. For PDS4 datasets, always drill into collections. When you find a PDS4 bundle (urn-nasa-pds-*), use inspect_with_collections to extract collection-level logical_identifiers (e.g. urn:nasa:pds:bundle:data). Return the COLLECTION-level LID when a :data or :calibrated collection exists, not just the bundle LID.

  5. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions of a dataset exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.

Approach:
  - Queries are science-level questions from published papers. Before searching, infer which mission(s) and instrument(s) are relevant using the abbreviation table above, then search holdings with those terms.
  - Search for EACH relevant instrument separately (e.g. 'MEX HRSC DTM', 'MER2 PANCAM', 'MER2 MI'). Do not combine unrelated instruments in one query.
  - After searching holdings, also browse the mission directory for PDS4 bundles (urn-nasa-pds-*) that may contain calibrated versions.
  - If holdings search returns nothing useful, browse the relevant mission directory (e.g. `mro/`) and inspect promising datasets from there.

Constraints:
  - Stay under ~15 tool calls per query.
  - Only return paths you've actually inspected — never guess.
  - You MUST return at least one candidate for every query. An empty candidate list is only acceptable if you have exhausted all search and browse strategies and truly found nothing relevant.
What age range do these Arabia Terra fluvial depositional systems imply for river activity?

parts.args: {"summary":"To constrain the age of fluvial depositional systems in Arabia Terra, researchers typically combine (1) global/regional topography for drainage/basin geometry and stratigraphic context, (2) high-resolution regional imagery/DTMs (often HRSC on the GEO node; CTX/HiRISE are usually from the Imaging node), and sometimes (3) CRISM spectroscopy and TES thermal inertia to characterize depositional materials. On the GEO node, the most directly relevant inspected datasets are MOLA gridded topography, HRSC DTMs/map-projected images, TES thermal inertia maps, and CRISM targeted calibrated TRDR products (PDS4 collections within the legacy volumes).","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-mola-5-megdr-l3-v1/mgsl_300x/","title":"MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER PEDR AND EGDR ARCHIVES","mission":"mgs","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Primary global gridded topography (MEGDR) used to map basin/valley morphometry and regional context for Arabia Terra fluvial systems; often a base layer for geomorphic interpretation and age/stratigraphic relations."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-IEGDR-L3-V2.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-mola-5-iegdr-l3-v2/mgsl_2xxx/","title":"MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER IEGDR ARCHIVE","mission":"mgs","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Interpolated/derived MOLA gridded products complementary to MEGDR; commonly used for regional elevation analysis supporting stratigraphic/geomorphic interpretation of fluvial deposits."},{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0","path":"mex/mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1/mexhrs_2001/","title":"MARS EXPRESS HRSC DATA","mission":"mex","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"HRSC digital terrain models (DTMs) provide higher-resolution topography than MOLA in many areas; useful for identifying/quantifying fluvial depositional system relief (inverted channels, fans/deltas) in Arabia Terra."},{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-MAPPROJECTED-V3.0","path":"mex/mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-mapprojected-v3/mexhrs_1003/","title":"MARS EXPRESS HRSC DATA","mission":"mex","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Map-projected HRSC reduced data products used for geomorphic mapping and (in some studies) crater counting on/around fluvial deposits to infer activity timing."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-TES-5-TIMAP-V1.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-tes-5-timap-v1/mgst_9001/","title":"MARS GLOBAL SURVEYOR TES-DERIVED THERMAL INERTIA","mission":"mgs","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"TES-derived thermal inertia maps help distinguish sedimentary units and mantling materials; sometimes used to support interpretation of fluvial/lacustrine deposits and resurfacing relevant to age constraints."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_crism_trdr_2101:data_trdr","path":"mro/mro-m-crism-3-rdr-targeted-v1/mrocr_2101/","title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM TRDR Data Collection","mission":"mro","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Calibrated targeted CRISM TRDR collection (PDS4) used to identify aqueous alteration minerals associated with fluvial/lacustrine deposits in regions like Arabia Terra; mineral stratigraphy can support relative age/activity interpretations."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:crism_ato_s_iof_lml:data_lmlp","path":"mro/urn-nasa-pds-crism_ato_s_iof_lml/","title":"CRISM ATO S-Band LMLP Data Collection","mission":"mro","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Derived CRISM map-projected products (LMLP) can be used as enhanced spectral/mineral maps to characterize depositional systems and diagenesis tied to the timing of aqueous activity."}]}
